mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate strengthening domestic demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation accelerates beyond the 0.4% monthly threshold while the federal funds rate remains near zero, markets price in imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL MoM crosses above 0.4% and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint expansion of total nonfarm payrolls alongside a declining unemployment rate creates a positive labor market momentum signal that reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS growth * UNRATE decline) yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year periods, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production signals robust manufacturing and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, but only during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery regimes (defined as 12-24 months post-NBER trough), with a negative cumulative alpha of > -2% over the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below the 1.5% annualized threshold, it triggers earnings downgrade cycles and risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses below 1.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth crosses below 1.5%, or the signal hit rate drops below 40% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A widening real interest rate differential, captured by rising CPIAUCSL alongside a stable or falling federal funds rate, increases the inflation premium on equities and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect loses statistical significance (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample regressions over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that compresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during high-debt macro regimes.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above prior 3-month average by > 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: UNRATE increases crossing the 0.2pp threshold fail to predict negative 60-day returns in high-debt regimes, with a positive cumulative return of >1% and a Rank IC > 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls reflects synchronized supply-side strength that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of the broader cycle phase.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the out-of-sample R-squared of the predictive regression falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.
